turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm is completely stalled at iteration 849 with gbest fitness 9.315e-10. No improvement for 200 iterations. Diversity is essentially zero (1.24e-20), all particles have zero velocity (velocity_zero_ratio=1.0), and all fitness values are identical (std=0). The swarm has collapsed to a single point very close to, but not exactly at, the global minimum of zero. | [DIAGNOSE] This is premature convergence despite the unimodal landscape. The swarm lost diversity completely before reaching the exact optimum, likely due to parameter settings that caused rapid contraction. With identical positions and zero velocity, the swarm cannot self-correct or refine further, leaving it stuck at a near-optimal but non-zero point. | [STRATEGIZE] The goal is to reintroduce minimal diversity and velocity to enable local refinement without losing the high-quality solution. A Gaussian mutation on a fraction of particles adds small, controlled perturbations, creating gradients that allow the swarm to fine-tune toward the true minimum. This is superior to resetting particles (which would lose the current good position) or Levy flights (designed for multimodal escape).

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The Gaussian mutation on 15 random particles with scale=0.0001 did not change the best fitness at all. This suggests either the perturbations were too small relative to the search space to create meaningful diversity, or they were applied to particles that were not the best, so the global best remained unchanged despite some particles moving. Since the swarm is completely collapsed with zero velocity, mutating random particles may not directly refine the global best position. A more effective approach is to apply a small perturbation directly to the best particle to locally search around it and possibly find a slightly better point. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The swarm has converged to a single point extremely close to but not at the global minimum. Due to complete diversity collapse, the swarm cannot self-improve. Standard PSO dynamics cannot operate because all gradients are effectively zero among identical particles. A localized mutation on the best particle is required to nudge it toward the exact optimum.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] Both Gaussian mutation attempts (random and best-targeted) failed to improve the global best fitness. The swarm remains completely stuck at a near-zero but non-optimal point. The mutations likely did not produce a position with lower fitness due to inappropriate scale or bad luck, but more fundamentally, the extreme collapse of diversity requires a stronger intervention to re-enable meaningful search. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The swarm has prematurely converged on a suboptimal point in this unimodal landscape. Its dynamics are frozen because all particles share identical position and zero velocity. Localized perturbations are too fragile; the whole swarm needs fresh random positions to restart the convergence process from a broader sample, which in a convex landscape will quickly refine to a better optimum.
